characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A team of professionals of the dream-sharing technology assembled and led by Dominic Cobb. They are paid by Japanese billionaire Saito to cause an "inception" — implanting an idea in the mind of Saito's rival billionaire Robert Fischer through dream-sharing. Anti-Hero: Both Cobb and Saito have rather selfish motives for messing with Robert Fischer's subconscious, and all the other team members are motivated by either money, curiosity, the thrill of the challenge, or a combination of all these.They're arguably altering Fischer's subconscious for the better, in that he'll have a more positive outlook on his father and life in general, but it's an outlook that was based on lies.Even if you put aside their selfish motives, the team's remarkably deceptive and manipulative methods are morally questionable, to say the least. Could be justifiable on Saito's behalf: when Saito explains the job to Cobb in Mombasa, he mentions that if Fischer's company isn't broken up then it will become a global energy monopoly, so by forcing the company to dissolve it would be preventing a possible global energy crisis. Badass Crew: Who knew that a team of dream-invaders could be this cool? While looking like pretty much anyone else in the real world, in the dream worlds that they infiltrate, they're a team of expert heisters. Badass in a Nice Suit: Everyone, though Arthur kicking ass and taking names in hisWaistcoat of Styleis a standout example. Breaking the Fellowship:Implied. But they seem to be going their separate ways at the end.

characters.name: The Team
characters.description: A team of professionals of the dream-sharing technology assembled and led by Dominic Cobb. They are paid by Japanese billionaire Saito to cause an "inception" — implanting an idea in the mind of Saito's rival billionaire Robert Fischer through dream-sharing. Anti-Hero: Both Cobb and Saito have rather selfish motives for messing with Robert Fischer's subconscious, and all the other team members are motivated by either money, curiosity, the thrill of the challenge, or a combination of all these.They're arguably altering Fischer's subconscious for the better, in that he'll have a more positive outlook on his father and life in general, but it's an outlook that was based on lies.Even if you put aside their selfish motives, the team's remarkably deceptive and manipulative methods are morally questionable, to say the least. Could be justifiable on Saito's behalf: when Saito explains the job to Cobb in Mombasa, he mentions that if Fischer's company isn't broken up then it will become a global energy monopoly, so by forcing the company to dissolve it would be preventing a possible global energy crisis. Badass Crew: Who knew that a team of dream-invaders could be this cool? While looking like pretty much anyone else in the real world, in the dream worlds that they infiltrate, they're a team of expert heisters. Badass in a Nice Suit: Everyone, though Arthur kicking ass and taking names in hisWaistcoat of Styleis a standout example. Breaking the Fellowship:Implied. But they seem to be going their separate ways at the end.

characters.name: Dominic "Dom" Cobb
characters.description: The Extractor. A master architect of dreams and the world's best thief of secrets within the dreams. Following a botched job for Cobol Engineering, he assembles and leads a crew to cause an inception at Saito's behest, but can't perform his architect duties due to being haunted by memories and projections of his deceased wife Mal. Ambiguous Situation:Did Cobb ever really get back to his family, or is he still dreaming? He spins the top totem to check, but doesn't watch it long enough to be sure, and the film ends with the top wobbling, but not falling. Anti-Hero: He certainly isn't a mean person, but he also doesn't hesitate to use the occasionalBatman Gambitto get what he needs, depending on the job. Author Avatar: Given the common interpretation that the entire film is an allegory for the film-making process, Cobb himself is clearly a stand-in for director Christopher Nolan and physically resembles him to a great extent. Badass Boast: He's the most skilled extractor, and had boasted as such.

characters.name: Arthur
characters.description: The Point-man. Dom Cobb's most trusted friend and partner in their extraction/inception schemes.Has a certain taste for the finer things in lifethat he can't help but bring with him to every job. Badass Bookworm: His job with the group is to do the research, and his hallway fight definitely pushes him into badass territory. Cobb mentions that he's in charge of doing the research, and he's smart enough to figure out how to simulate falling in zero gravity. He's also badass enough to pull it off, in limited time, while fighting off mooks. Again, in zero gravity. Butt-Monkey: Poor Arthur. He gets shot twice at the beginning. Once just to cause him pain, and once BY HIS OWN TEAMMATE (admittedly to wake him up). When it comes to testing out the "kicks", somehow Arthur's always the one getting his chair knocked over, his gun dissed by Eames, and so on. His awesomeness at the end makes up for it, though. The Comically Serious: Arthur is the most level-headed and serious member of the team, and as such is not amused by Eames' somewhat careless behavior, getting his chair constantly tipped over, or loud music. Consummate Professional: He has an intense professionalism and intolerance to the lack of detail that goes into the inception job.

characters.name: Saito
characters.description: The Tourist. A Japanese billionaire and CEO of Proclus Global, Dom Cobb was hired to extract informations from on behalf of rival company Cobol Engineering. It didn't work out, and Saito ended up hiring Cobb to cause an inception in the mind of rival billionaire Robert Fischer in order to dismantle his company. He spares no expense to that end, and demands to be part of the inception crew to make sure they get the job done. Affably Evil: While Saito is a shady businessman, he also remains polite to his employees and sticks to his end of the bargain. Business Rival Sabotage: Saito hires Dom Cobb and Arthur to perform "inception" on Robert Fischer, the son and heir of his competitor Maurice Fischer, to subconsciously plant the idea of dissolving Maurice's company Fischer Morrow. The Chessmaster: Saito allowed Cobb and Arthur to infiltrate his mind at the start of the film as an "audition" for the Fischer job, as well as finding out enough about Cobb to get the perfect lure to make sure he accepts Saito's offer. Corrupt Corporate Executive: He hired the team to break apart his rival's company usingMind Rapetechniques. However, given how said rival is equally corrupt, Saito's motives can be seen in a more sympathetic light. Andhe also honors the agreementhe makes with Cobb.

characters.name: Ariadne
characters.description: The Architect. The most brilliant student of Professor Stephen Miles, she's chosen by Dom Cobb as the team's architect of dreams, though unknowingly to the rest of the team, is also Cobb's tether. Audience Surrogate: In a refreshing twist on the trope, she doesn't just sit there andask questions so that the more experienced characters can provide exposition. She catches on to the idea of dream-sharing rather quickly, and makes an effort to sway Cobb from his self-destructive course. Not to mention that it'sheridea togo into the fourth levelafter Cobb and Eames have basically given up. In short, she's a character who behaves exactly as the audience likes to believe they would in that situation. Badass Bookworm: Designs all the dream levels, complex enough that if Mal hadn't showed up to screw everything up, the mission most likely would have gone smoothly. Brainy Brunette: All the level designs are a result of her talent as an architect. Corrupt the Cutie: Professor Miles even refers to Cobb as wanting to "corrupt" one of his best students. Cobb openly tells her the job is not legal, but he, Arthur, and Ariadne herself each admit that after tasting the power of dream architecture, she would never want to go back. She willingly joinsThe Team.

characters.name: Eames
characters.description: The Forger. A professional thief, shapeshifter within the dreams and forger with connections. Ambiguously Gay: Eames calls Arthur "darling" and flirts with Saito (as well as Fischer, although that one is a part of the job) in a girl's form. The Big Guy: When the team encounters a problem, his solution is to pull out a grenade launcher. Collector of Forms: Can shapeshift in dreams; however, he indicates that he needs to examine a target closely first before he can accurately assume their form. When preparing for the mission, he sneaks himself into the offices of Fischer Morrow in order to study Christopher Browning up close and learn his mannerisms so he can later pose as him. Because this ability is dependent on memory and acting ability, Eames can't impersonate any of the projections sent after the team, but he does have a few shapes he can recall from past jobs, allowing him to become "a lovely young lady I've used before" while stealing Fischer's wallet in the second level. Deadpan Snarker: The biggest one in the film. The majority of his quips are directed at Arthur, but Saito, Cobb, and Yusuf all get burned by Eames at one point or another.Arthur: Eames, I am impressed.Eames: Your condescension, as always, is much appreciated, Arthur.

characters.name: Yusuf
characters.description: The Chemist. He specializes in the various sedating substances that are needed for the dream-sharing technology. Badass Driver: In the first layer, he has to continue driving the van and perform the kick while the projections are trying to kill them. Bollywood Nerd: A cool nerd, though, and one who can handle himself in the heat of action, which is key considering his dream is the top layer. Good Old Ways: His old-fashioned apothecary. Meaningful Name: Yusuf is the Arabic variant of Joseph (the one who famously interpreted Pharaoh's dreams inThe Bible).

characters.name: Malorie "Mal" Cobb
characters.description: The Shade. Dom Cobb's beloved late wife, who committed suicide. Cobb is still haunted by her as both of them went too far in the use of the dream-sharing technology. As a result, Cobb's subconscious produces projections of Mal within the dreams, and those often end up real nuisances to his extraction/inception missions. Anti-Villain:The projection of Mal only wants to have Cobb all to herself but sabotages every dream mission he does. Ax-Crazy: She's a dangerous lunatic who kills several people to get to Cobb. Became Their Own Antithesis:Played for Dramawith the real Mal.She became addicted to dreaming in Limbo and didn't want to leave, voluntarily repressing her own totem. After Cobb performed inception on her, planting the idea that she had to wake up, Mal becameobsessedwith escaping the dream long after she'd already done so, unaware that she was awake.From a subjective perspective, theAx-Crazy, ruthless and homicidal projection of Mal which haunts Cobb in the dream worlds is apparently a far cry from what the real Mal was likebefore her inception, described by Arthur as the kindest woman he and Cobb knew. Played for Dramawith the real Mal.She became addicted to dreaming in Limbo and didn't want to leave, voluntarily repressing her own totem. After Cobb performed inception on her, planting the idea that she had to wake up, Mal becameobsessedwith escaping the dream long after she'd already done so, unaware that she was awake.

characters.name: Robert Michael Fischer Jr.
characters.description: The Mark. The son of billionaire Maurice Fischer. After inheriting his father's company and assets, he becomes de facto a rival to Saito, who hires Dom Cobb and his crew to perform an inception in the mind of Robert to have him dismantle his company. Anti-Villain: Type IV. He's only a "villain" in so far as he's inheriting his father's legacy of creating a monopoly and his subconscious generates militarized projections. Crazy-Prepared: Cobb and his team are surprised to find out he has trained his subconscious to generate militarized projections in case an extraction is attempted on him, which makes the inception mission considerably more difficult. Deadpan Snarker: Upon realizing he's been kidnapped on the first dream level, Fischer just lets out a resigned "wonderful". Fiction 500: Even more than Saito. He's so rich that should he not break up his company it will become an energy monopoly that controls half the world, and that's not even counting his off-hand comments about his wallet being worth more than $500 and him being insured for $10 million in the case of kidnapping.

characters.name: Professor Stephen Miles
characters.description: Mal's father and a professor of real life architecture Dom Cobb learned a lot from to build dream settings. Despite not approving of Cobb's lifestyle as a thief, he nonetheless supports him when he can. Cool Old Guy: While not entirely approving, he's fully privy to Cobb's criminal lifestyle, and even provides him withaccomplicesto help him when he can. The Mentor: He was this to Cobb. Presumably Mal as well, given her knowledge of the dream world. Outliving One's Offspring: His daughter Mal committed suicide before the events of the film. The Professor: A professor in Architecture who somehow knows about the dream mechanics, and was the one who taught Cobb and Mal everything they know. He's also Ariadne's teacher.

characters.name: Maurice Fischer
characters.description: A billionaire and CEO of Fischer Morrow Energy Conglomerate, Saito's primary competitor. Dying and bedridden, his son Robert is set to take up the empire upon his death. Ambiguous Situation: He never had a good relationship with his son, but there is enough missing information that it's possible he did still love his son deep down. His last word to Robert was "disappointed", which Robert interpreted to mean his father was disappointed his son couldn't live up to the family.Cobb and his team plant the suggestion that his father in reality was disappointed that Robert tried to be like him and that Maurice wanted his son to be his own person. Corrupt Corporate Executive: Implied, though due to his very limited time on screen it's not confirmed. Disappointed in You: Zigzagged. Maurice was cold and distant to his own son all the way up to his death bed, his last word being "disappointed".In the dream, Cobb and his team turn it on its head so that Robert can have his desired reconciliation, with Maurice's projection revealing that he's disappointed that his son tried to be like him, not that he couldn't. Jerkass: Towards his son at least.

characters.name: Peter Browning
characters.description: Robert's godfather and Maurice's longtime friend and legal counsel. With Maurice dying, Peter has been scrambling for more and more power within the company. Eames impersonates him within Robert's dream in order to turn Robert against him. Abduction Is Love: Eames as Browning uses this as justification as part of their gambit. Corrupt Corporate Executive: Whereas it's implied with Maurice, he's outright stated to be the fact for Browning. The sicker Maurice becomes, the more powerful Browning becomes. Eames even outright calls him a "vulture." Even Evil Has Loved Ones: Browning is a power-grubbingCorrupt Corporate Executivewho treats his associates like crap, but he seems to legitimately care about Robert. Honorary Uncle: Robert even calls him "Uncle Peter."

characters.name: Nash
characters.description: The architect of dreams who was hired by Dom Cobb for the extraction mission that targeted Saito. He gets replaced by Ariadne later on as his mistakes made him unreliable. Dirty Coward: Believing that Cobol will have them all killed, he ran to Saito for protection, selling out Cobb and Arthur. Unfortunately Saito had his own agenda. In-Universe Factoid Failure: Nash creates a dream replica of Saito's "love nest", but forgets details like what the carpet's made out of. This lets Saito know he's still in a dream. Rewarded as a Traitor Deserves: Saito offers Cobb and Arthur the chance to kill him, but they decline. Saito claims his men won't harm him, butwhat Cobol will do is another matter. Sacrificial Lamb: His leaving early gives way to Ariadne getting hired by Cobb.
